param.name: mass_tolerance_ppm
param.label: Mass tolerance [ppm]
param.help: Mass tolerance in ppm for database matching.

param.name: time_tolerance
param.label: Retention time tolerance [s]
param.help: Retention time tolerance in seconds for finding peaks derived from the same parent compound.

param.name: max_diff_rt
param.label: Maximum RT difference [s]
param.help: Maximum retention time difference allowed between peaks that are considered as potential adducts/isotopes of the same compound.

param.name: correlation_threshold
param.label: Correlation threshold
param.help: Correlation threshold between peaks to qualify as adducts/isotopes of the same metabolite.

param.name: corthresh
param.label: Correlation threshold
param.help: Minimum correlation required for peaks to be considered as supporting the same chemical identity. Peaks above this threshold contribute to the chemscore calculation.

param.name: min_cluster_size
param.label: Minimum cluster size
param.help: The minimum number of nodes to be considered as a cluster.

param.name: deep_split
param.label: Deep split
param.help: Deep split provides a rough control over sensitivity to cluster splitting. The higher the value, the more and smaller clusters will be produced (see WGCNA package documentation).

param.name: network_type
param.label: Network type
param.help: Network type parameter affects how the network's adjacency matrix is created from the correlation matrix (see WGCNA package documentation).

param.name: annotation
param.label: Annotation table
param.help: Annotation table from upstream tool. Contains compound annotations with chemical_ID, mz, rt, and other relevant columns.

param.name: adduct_weights
param.label: Adduct weights table
param.help: A weight-by-adduct table used for scoring annotations.

param.name: compound_table
param.label: Compound database
param.help: Database of compounds for annotation. Required columns: compound_id, monoisotopic_mass, and molecular_formula.

param.name: adduct_table
param.label: Adduct database (optional)
param.help: Database of adducts. If provided, compound-adduct pairs will be generated. Required columns: adduct, charge, mass, and n_molecules.

param.name: peak_table
param.label: Peak table
param.help: Peak table containing peak identifiers, m/z, retention time, and optionally intensity values, module assignments, RT clusters, and mass defect.

param.name: peak_correlation_matrix
param.label: Peak correlation matrix
param.help: Correlation matrix computed by the Compute Peak Correlations tool. Square matrix with peak-to-peak correlations.

param.name: global_cor
param.label: Global correlation matrix
param.help: Global correlation data from Reformat Annotation tool. Provides context for correlation-based scoring.

param.name: metadata_table
param.label: Peak metadata table
param.help: Peak metadata table from recetox-aplcms or similar tool. Required columns: id, mz, rt.

param.name: input_table
param.label: Input table
param.help: Input table containing peaks. Can be either: - A peak table with columns: peak, mz, rt (and optionally other intensity columns) - An annotation table with mz column

param.name: chemCompMz
param.label: Compound-pathway database
param.help: Database linking compounds to metabolic pathways. Should contain: - chemical_ID: Compound identifier (matching annotation table) - Pathway information - m/z values for compounds Can be HMDB, KEGG, or custom pathway database.

param.name: mass_defect_precision
param.label: Precision for computing mass defect
param.help: Number of decimal places to round mass defect values.

param.name: peak_rt_width
param.label: Estimated chromatographic peak width
param.help: Expected chromatographic peak width in seconds for RT clustering.

param.name: intensity_deviation_tolerance
param.label: Intensity deviation tolerance
param.help: Maximum allowed deviation from theoretical isotope intensity ratios (default: 0.1 = ±10%). For example, if the theoretical M+1 intensity is 10% of M, observed intensities between 9% and 11% would be accepted with default settings.

param.name: mass_defect_tolerance
param.label: Mass defect tolerance
param.help: Maximum allowed difference in mass defect between observed and theoretical isotope. Helps filter false positives from co-eluting compounds.

param.name: maximum_isotopes
param.label: Maximum isotopes
param.help: Maximum number of isotopes to consider for each compound.

param.name: min_ions_per_chemical
param.label: Minimum ions per chemical
param.help: Minimum number of adducts/isotopes required for a high-confidence match.

param.name: pathwaycheckmode
param.label: Pathway check mode
param.help: Mode for pathway analysis and score boosting.

param.name: db_name
param.label: Database name
param.help: Name of the pathway database being used.
param.option: KEGG (Kyoto Encyclopedia of Genes and Genomes)

param.name: metadata_table
param.label: Metadata table
param.help: Peak metadata table*.

param.name: intensity_table
param.label: Intensity table
param.help: Table with intensities** for features (rows) across samples (columns).

param.name: compound_table
param.label: Compound database
param.help: Database of compounds according to which the annotation is performed. The database is required to contain the fields compound_id, monoisotopic_mass, and molecular_formula.

param.name: adduct_table
param.label: Adduct database
param.help: Database of adduct which is combined with the database of compound to form a molecule-adduct pairs. The database is required to contain adduct, charge, mass, and n_molecules.

param.name: adduct_weights
param.label: Adduct weights
param.help: A weight-by-adduct table.

param.name: mass_tolerance_ppm
param.label: Mass tolerance [ppm]
param.help: Mass tolerance in ppm for database matching.
